comments.body: HANDOFF SNAPSHOT (2026-07-08). LANDED on main via Gerrit (all MERGED): 389 A1 b306, 390 A2 36d1, 407 A3 34b1 (tooling only), 408 C4 943f, 409 C1/C3 a713/acd0, 410 close-out (RC1 resurrection test + RC2b .bak-restart test + fsck resurrection-repair edge fix + doc GC link). CLOSED w/ completion signatures (5): b306, 36d1, 943f, a713, acd0. GC follow-up polite-antivirus-bedbug (536b, idea) created+linked. OPEN/REMAINING (6): 34b1 A3 (in_progress; tooling landed, needs 4 tests + no-in-flight-pass check + LIVE remediation run w/ recorded counts), c244 C2 (open; root-caused, unimplemented), a118 B1 (open; live shadow+heal), d01e D1 (open; live matrix), 9622 (open; NO plan-review), eca4 (open; NO plan-review). Recommended order: A3 -> C2 -> B1 -> D1 -> 9622 -> eca4 (eca4 last, biggest conflict surface). Live infra confirmed (JIRA env + acli + project REB); user authorized live runs. Epic stays in_progress until all 6 close.

compiled_state.description:
# Jira reconciler correctness & the ticket-store corruption it caused

Broadened from the original store-only charter (f193 follow-ups). Root-cause work (bug
saggy-pupil-plant / f193 and the 2026-07-07 reconciler deep-dive, session log
spirited-balsamic-sealion) established the throughline: **the Jira reconciler is the epicenter**.
Its 20-min CI pass is the busiest concurrent writer of the `tickets` branch, and its shallow-fetch +
rebase workflow (RC1) *caused* the ~2,400-issue store corruption. Four bands:
(A) store hardening + remediation, (B) convergence completion, (C) latent-gap closure,
(D) comprehensive live validation.

## Context (current state)
- **RC1 (fixed, #1):** reconciler CI used shallow `--depth=1` fetch + `git rebase`, resurrecting
  compaction-deleted source files -> `SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT` (commit 5a7548a). Historical damage remains.
- **RC2b (band A):** a concurrent write on a stale base gets an HLC below a peer's snapshot horizon; a
  clean union-merge keeps it, but replay's positional skip drops it -> silent data loss.
- **Invariant gap (band A):** docs I1 says compaction RENAMEs folded files to `*.retired`; the code hard-`os.remove()`s them.
- **Convergence redesign (landed, epic file-lens-jewel; band B):** three-way-merge baseline + binding
  lifecycle (ADRs 0026-0029, 0031) shipped, but the per-binding baseline runs dual-write SHADOW — the
  Phase-3 consumer swap is not yet live, and two arbiters encode the rule twice.
- **Latent reconciler gaps (band C):** silent same-field clobber with no conflict signal; hard-delete
  -> re-create unreachable + false comment; silent field-allowlist drop; coarse bulk-path 429 handling.
- **Validation gap (band D):** correctness is argued from code + an OSS/commercial comparison but not
  PROVEN end-to-end against live Jira. Live validation is performed by an AGENT (ACLI access), with
  guaranteed cleanup of test artifacts in both Jira and the local store — not deferred to the operator.
- Design as a whole matches/beats OSS + commercial-tier peers. Single-hop status transitions are ACCEPTED / out of scope.

## Success Criteria (target state)
- **A. Store hardened + remediated:** compaction non-destructive (I1 `*.retired`); no concurrent
  sub-horizon append silently dropped (RC2b); live-store `rebar fsck` reports zero
  SNAPSHOT_INCONSISTENT / ORPHAN_EVENT / MISSING_CREATE.
- **B. Convergence fully live:** the per-binding baseline is the authoritative direction arbiter
  (Phase-3 consumer swap flipped + validated on live passes).
- **C. Latent gaps closed:** true both-sides field conflicts recorded; hard-delete -> re-create works;
  field-allowlist drops observable; bulk-path 429 honors Retry-After.
- **D. Proven live:** the full bidirectional surface is validated against live Jira by an agent, with
  GUARANTEED cleanup of every test artifact in both Jira and the local store.

## Scope boundary
This epic is reconciler **correctness + validation**. Reconciler **maintainability/refactor** work —
oversized-function extraction (fusible-mean-porcupine), central git adapter (sewable-authorial-eagle),
vendor-adapter seam (ambery-tweed-grosbeak), invariant detect/remediate split
(inherited-genealogic-haddock), config split (seedy-edema-groan) — lives under the Janitor Cleanup
epic, NOT here. Separate reconciler epics also stay separate: GitHub parity (slimy-tuber-stoop),
authenticated identity (gnu-whale-ichor), INDETERMINATE/LLM-gate handling (jira-reb-687).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Band A children landed: A1 (`*.retired`), A2 (RC2b horizon-safe; carries an approach decision), A3 (live-store remediation to zero fsck).
- [ ] Band B: B1 (Phase-3 baseline consumer swap; agent performs its live validation with cleanup).
- [ ] Band C children landed: C1 (clobber conflict signal), C2 (hard-delete re-create), C3 (field-allowlist alert), C4 (bulk-path 429 parity).
- [ ] Band D: D1 (comprehensive live validation + guaranteed bilateral cleanup).
- [ ] Dependency edges wired: A1->A2, A1->A3, A2->A3; C1 relates_to B1; D1 relates_to B1/C1/C2/C3/C4.
- [ ] Risk #5 (single-hop status transitions) explicitly accepted / out of scope.


## Cross-child coordination (verified in plan review)
- SEQUENCING: A3's live remediation pauses the reconciler, which resets B1's >=10-consecutive-clean-shadow-pass
  streak. Do NOT run A3's live pass during B1's streak window — sequence them.
- SHARED FILE: C1 (feline-wandering-dassierat, `apply_outbound.py` conflict leaf ~207-232) and C3
  (nonglacial-tense-vulture, `apply_outbound.py` ~117-124) edit the same file in different regions — coordinate merge order (relates_to linked).
- VALIDATION ORDER: D1 (close-ferreous-cardinal) validates the POST-FIX behavior and runs AFTER the C-band lands;
  captured in D1's body rather than as hard depends_on edges to avoid blocking-link promotion across mixed-type siblings.
compiled_state.title: Jira reconciler correctness & the ticket-store corruption it caused — convergence completion, latent-gap closure, and store hardening + remediation (f193 follow-ups)
